items.name: Sleep
items.description: 7-9 hours of consistent, quality sleep per night
items.best_for: Memory consolidation, immune function, cognitive performance
items.developer_impact: 30% cognitive impairment after one bad night; chronic sleep debt doubles bug rate
items.quick_fix: Consistent wake time + dark room + no screens 1 hour before bed

items.name: Exercise
items.description: 150+ minutes of moderate exercise per week
items.best_for: Focus, stress reduction, cardiovascular health, mood
items.developer_impact: Improves focus for 2-3 hours post-exercise; reduces sitting-related health risks
items.quick_fix: Morning walk or jog before work; standing desk for afternoon

items.name: Nutrition
items.description: Balanced meals with protein, complex carbs, and healthy fats
items.best_for: Sustained energy, cognitive function, mood stability
items.minimum_effective_dose: Protein at breakfast, water throughout day, no sugar crashes
items.developer_impact: Sugar crashes cause afternoon brain fog; dehydration reduces cognition 25%
items.quick_fix: Eggs/nuts for breakfast, water bottle on desk, avoid vending machine

items.name: Ergonomics
items.description: Proper workspace setup to prevent chronic pain and injury
items.minimum_effective_dose: Monitor at eye level, chair with lumbar support, hourly movement
items.developer_impact: RSI, back pain, and eye strain are top causes of developer disability
items.quick_fix: Raise monitor to eye level, set hourly posture reminder, 20-20-20 eye rule

items.name: Social Connection
items.description: Regular meaningful interactions with friends, family, and colleagues
items.best_for: Mental health, stress resilience, sense of belonging
items.minimum_effective_dose: 1 meaningful conversation per day, 1 social activity per week
items.developer_impact: Isolation increases depression risk; social support buffers work stress
items.quick_fix: Lunch with a colleague, weekly call with friend, join a developer community

comparisons.insight: These five areas are interconnected: poor sleep ruins exercise motivation, bad nutrition reduces focus, poor ergonomics causes pain that disrupts sleep. Start with sleep — it's the foundation that makes everything else possible.

keyInsight: Sleep, exercise, nutrition, ergonomics, and social connection are the five foundations of developer health. They're interconnected — fix sleep first (it enables everything else), then add exercise (it improves focus and sleep), then optimize nutrition and ergonomics.
